agent_output:
{
  "domain_analysis": {
    "business_context": "A logistics platform managing parcel delivery through a defined shipment lifecycle, with weight-based pricing tiers. The system must enforce state machine transitions and physical constraints on parcels.",
    "bounded_context": "Shipment Management — covers shipment creation, lifecycle state transitions, weight validation, and pricing classification.",
    "ubiquitous_language": [
      { "term": "Shipment", "definition": "A parcel delivery unit created with a pickup and delivery address, progressing through defined lifecycle states." },
      { "term": "Pickup Address", "definition": "The origin location where the parcel is collected from the sender." },
      { "term": "Delivery Address", "definition": "The destination location where the parcel is delivered to the recipient." },
      { "term": "Pending", "definition": "Initial shipment state after creation, before the parcel enters transit." },
      { "term": "In Transit", "definition": "Shipment state indicating the parcel is actively being transported from pickup to delivery address." },
      { "term": "Delivered", "definition": "Terminal shipment state indicating the parcel has been successfully handed off at the delivery address. Irreversible." },
      { "term": "Weight Bracket", "definition": "A pricing classification tier determined by the parcel's weight: Standard (0–10 kg), Heavy (10–50 kg), or Freight (>50 kg)." },
      { "term": "Standard", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels weighing greater than 0 kg up to and including 10 kg." },
      { "term": "Heavy", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels weighing greater than 10 kg up to and including 50 kg." },
      { "term": "Freight", "definition": "Weight bracket for parcels weighing greater than 50 kg up to and including 1000 kg." }
    ]
  },

  "business_rules": [
    {
      "id": "BR-1",
      "rule": "A shipment can only be created when both a pickup address and a delivery address are provided.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "A shipment without both endpoints is physically meaningless — it cannot be routed, priced, or fulfilled. This is a data integrity constraint.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any attempt to create a shipment with a missing or empty pickup address or delivery address must be rejected."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-2",
      "rule": "A shipment in the 'In Transit' state cannot transition back to 'Pending'.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "State machine integrity: transit represents a physical event (parcel was picked up and is moving). Reverting to pending would corrupt the audit trail and misrepresent physical reality.",
      "testable_assertion": "Attempting to set a shipment's state from 'In Transit' to 'Pending' must be rejected with an error."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-3",
      "rule": "A shipment in the 'Delivered' state is terminal — no further state transitions are allowed.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "Delivery is a real-world finality. The parcel has been handed off. Allowing state changes after delivery would corrupt billing, SLA tracking, and chain-of-custody records.",
      "testable_assertion": "Attempting to change the state of a 'Delivered' shipment to any other state must be rejected with an error."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-4",
      "rule": "Shipment weight must be strictly greater than 0 kg.",
      "invariant": true,
      "invariant_justification": "A parcel with zero or negative weight is physically impossible. Allowing it would corrupt weight-based pricing and downstream logistics calculations.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any shipment with weight <= 0 must be rejected at creation and at any weight update."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-5",
      "rule": "Shipment weight must not exceed 1000 kg.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "The 1000 kg cap is a business policy reflecting current fleet/infrastructure limits. A VP could raise this limit if the company acquires heavier-duty vehicles or partners with a freight carrier.",
      "testable_assertion": "Any shipment with weight > 1000 kg must be rejected at creation and at any weight update."
    },
    {
      "id": "BR-6",
      "rule": "Weight bracket classification: Standard = weight in (0, 10] kg; Heavy = weight in (10, 50] kg; Freight = weight in (50, 1000] kg.",
      "invariant": false,
      "invariant_justification": "Bracket thresholds are pricing policy decisions. The business could restructure tiers (e.g., add an 'Ultra-Heavy' bracket or shift boundaries) without corrupting system state.",
      "testable_assertion": "A 10 kg shipment is classified as Standard; a 10.01 kg shipment is classified as Heavy; a 50 kg shipment is classified as Heavy; a 50.01 kg shipment is classified as Freight."
    }
  ],

  "acceptance_criteria": [
    {
      "id": "AC-1",
      "given": "A request to create a shipment with a valid pickup address and a valid delivery address",
      "when": "The shipment is created",
      "then": "The shipment is persisted in 'Pending' state with both addresses recorded"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-2",
      "given": "A request to create a shipment missing a pickup address",
      "when": "The creation is attempted",
      "then": "The system rejects the request and no shipment is created"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-3",
      "given": "A request to create a shipment missing a delivery address",
      "when": "The creation is attempted",
      "then": "The system rejects the request and no shipment is created"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-4",
      "given": "A shipment in 'Pending' state",
      "when": "The shipment transitions to 'In Transit'",
      "then": "The state change is accepted and the shipment is now 'In Transit'"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-5",
      "given": "A shipment in 'In Transit' state",
      "when": "An attempt is made to transition it back to 'Pending'",
      "then": "The transition is rejected and the shipment remains 'In Transit'"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-6",
      "given": "A shipment in 'In Transit' state",
      "when": "The shipment transitions to 'Delivered'",
      "then": "The state change is accepted and the shipment is now 'Delivered'"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-7",
      "given": "A shipment in 'Delivered' state",
      "when": "An attempt is made to transition it to any other state",
      "then": "The transition is rejected and the shipment remains 'Delivered'"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-8",
      "given": "A shipment with weight of exactly 10 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is calculated",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as 'Standard'"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-9",
      "given": "A shipment with weight of 10.01 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is calculated",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as 'Heavy'"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-10",
      "given": "A shipment with weight of exactly 50 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is calculated",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as 'Heavy'"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-11",
      "given": "A shipment with weight of 50.01 kg",
      "when": "The weight bracket is calculated",
      "then": "The shipment is classified as 'Freight'"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-12",
      "given": "A request to create a shipment with weight of 0 kg or less",
      "when": "The creation is attempted",
      "then": "The system rejects the request and no shipment is created"
    },
    {
      "id": "AC-13",
      "given": "A request to create a shipment with weight exceeding 1000 kg",
      "when": "The creation is attempted",
      "then": "The system rejects the request and no shipment is created"
    }
  ],

  "edge_cases": [
    { "scenario": "Shipment created with weight of exactly 0 kg", "expected_behavior": "Rejected — weight must be strictly positive." },
    { "scenario": "Shipment created with negative weight (e.g., -5 kg)", "expected_behavior": "Rejected — weight must be strictly positive." },
    { "scenario": "Shipment created with weight of exactly 1000 kg", "expected_behavior": "Accepted — 1000 kg is the upper bound, classified as Freight." },
    { "scenario": "Shipment created with weight of 1000.01 kg", "expected_behavior": "Rejected — exceeds the maximum weight limit." },
    { "scenario": "Weight at exact bracket boundary: 10 kg", "expected_behavior": "Classified as Standard (upper-inclusive: 0–10 kg)." },
    { "scenario": "Weight at exact bracket boundary: 50 kg", "expected_behavior": "Classified as Heavy (upper-inclusive: 10–50 kg)." },
    { "scenario": "Attempt to transition a 'Pending' shipment directly to 'Delivered'", "expected_behavior": "Spec is ambiguous — see escalation. Likely should be rejected since it skips the In Transit state, but the PM did not explicitly define this." },
    { "scenario": "Attempt to transition a 'Delivered' shipment to 'Delivered' again (idempotent delivery)", "expected_behavior": "Rejected — Delivered is terminal, no state transitions are allowed, including self-transitions." },
    { "scenario": "Pickup and delivery addresses are identical", "expected_behavior": "Spec is silent. The system should likely accept it (same-building transfers are valid in logistics), but the PM should confirm." },
    { "scenario": "Weight is updated after shipment is In Transit or Delivered", "expected_behavior": "Spec is silent on weight mutability after creation. If weight can change (e.g., weigh-in correction), bracket must be recalculated. If Delivered, this may conflict with finality." }
  ],

  "business_impact": {
    "financial": "Weight bracket classification directly drives pricing. Incorrect bracket assignment causes revenue leakage (undercharging) or customer disputes (overcharging). The bracket boundaries at 10 kg and 50 kg are critical billing thresholds.",
    "operational": "State machine enforcement prevents operational chaos — e.g., a parcel shown as Pending when it's actually in transit causes missed SLA alerts and incorrect driver dispatch. The 1000 kg cap protects fleet capacity planning.",
    "user": "Customers expect accurate shipment tracking. Incorrect state transitions erode trust. Clear weight limits set expectations at booking time, preventing failed pickups due to overweight parcels.",
    "risk": "Primary risks: (1) bracket boundary bugs causing systematic mis-pricing, (2) state machine violations corrupting audit trails needed for insurance claims and disputes, (3) ambiguous Pending-to-Delivered transition path could allow shipments to be marked delivered without transit tracking.",
    "stakeholders_affected": [
      { "group": "Customers (senders)", "impact": "Pricing accuracy, shipment visibility, and trust in delivery status." },
      { "group": "Recipients", "impact": "Delivery expectations set by shipment state accuracy." },
      { "group": "Finance / Billing", "impact": "Revenue integrity depends on correct weight bracket classification." },
      { "group": "Operations / Dispatch", "impact": "Driver routing and SLA monitoring depend on accurate state transitions." },
      { "group": "Customer Support", "impact": "Dispute resolution requires trustworthy state history and weight records." }
    ]
  },

  "confidence": {
    "level": 72,
    "reasoning": "The spec is clear on the core state machine rules, weight constraints, and bracket definitions. However, it is incomplete: the full set of valid state transitions is not explicitly enumerated (only two restrictions are stated), weight mutability after creation is undefined, and address validation rules are absent. These gaps are manageable but require PM clarification before implementation can be considered fully specified.",
    "high_confidence_areas": [
      "In Transit cannot revert to Pending (explicitly stated)",
      "Delivered is a terminal state (explicitly stated)",
      "Weight must be positive and <= 1000 kg (explicitly stated)",
      "Three weight brackets with boundaries at 10 and 50 kg (explicitly stated)",
      "Shipment creation requires both pickup and delivery address (explicitly stated)"
    ],
    "low_confidence_areas": [
      "Whether Pending can transition directly to Delivered (skipping In Transit)",
      "Whether weight can be modified after shipment creation",
      "Whether there are additional states beyond Pending, In Transit, and Delivered (e.g., Cancelled, Failed Delivery, Returned)",
      "Address validation rules (format, geographic constraints)",
      "Whether bracket boundaries are upper-inclusive or lower-inclusive (assumed upper-inclusive based on '0-10 is standard')"
    ],
    "assumptions": [
      "The only states are Pending, In Transit, and Delivered",
      "Initial state on creation is Pending",
      "Weight bracket boundaries are upper-inclusive: Standard = (0, 10], Heavy = (10, 50], Freight = (50, 1000]",
      "Weight is set at shipment creation and the spec does not address subsequent modifications",
      "Addresses are non-empty strings; no further format validation is specified"
    ]
  },

  "escalations": [
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Can a shipment transition directly from Pending to Delivered, skipping In Transit? The PM only stated that In Transit cannot go back to Pending and Delivered is terminal, but did not define the complete set of valid transitions.",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Operations / Dispatch", "Customers (senders)", "Recipients"],
      "options": [
        "A) Only allow Pending -> In Transit -> Delivered (strict linear lifecycle)",
        "B) Allow Pending -> Delivered as a shortcut (e.g., for same-day hand-delivery or walk-in pickup)",
        "C) Allow Pending -> In Transit -> Delivered AND Pending -> Delivered with different audit requirements"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — strict linear lifecycle. In logistics, skipping transit creates audit gaps and breaks chain-of-custody tracking. If walk-in scenarios exist, they should still pass through In Transit, even momentarily."
    },
    {
      "type": "missing_context",
      "description": "No cancellation or exception states are defined. Real-world logistics requires handling failed deliveries, returns, and cancellations. What happens when a delivery attempt fails or a customer cancels?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Customers (senders)", "Recipients", "Operations / Dispatch", "Customer Support"],
      "options": [
        "A) Add Cancelled state (reachable from Pending and possibly In Transit)",
        "B) Add Failed Delivery and Returned states for post-transit exceptions",
        "C) Defer exception handling to a future iteration and enforce only the three stated states for now"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option C for MVP, but flag this as a known gap. A production logistics system without cancellation handling will generate support tickets quickly."
    },
    {
      "type": "ambiguity",
      "description": "Weight bracket boundary inclusivity is not explicitly defined. The PM said '0-10 kg is standard, 10-50 kg is heavy, over 50 kg is freight' — but what classification does exactly 10 kg or exactly 50 kg receive?",
      "affected_stakeholders": ["Finance / Billing", "Customers (senders)"],
      "options": [
        "A) Upper-inclusive: 10 kg = Standard, 50 kg = Heavy (boundaries belong to the lower bracket)",
        "B) Lower-inclusive: 10 kg = Heavy, 50 kg = Freight (boundaries belong to the upper bracket)"
      ],
      "recommendation": "Option A — '0-10 kg is standard' most naturally reads as 10 kg being included in Standard. Confirm with PM to avoid billing disputes at boundary weights."
    }
  ],

  "rejection_reasons": []
}
